title: How to Maintain a Healthy Routine While Traveling

interlinking_suggestions.title: How to Build a Morning Routine for Freelance Success
How to Stay Healthy and Fit While Traveling
How to Integrate Calendly with Your Website

author.name: Isabelle Rossi
author.bio: Having lived and worked in over 30 countries, Isabelle is a leading voice on the digital nomad movement. She covers everything from visa strategies and travel hacking to maintaining well-being on the road.

key_takeaways: Treat your well-being as a critical business deliverable by proactively blocking time for non-negotiable health routines in your calendar before accepting any client meetings.
Ruthlessly eliminate non-essential choices, such as creating a work 'uniform' and pre-deciding meals, to preserve your finite mental energy for high-value tasks.
Mitigate the catastrophic risk of burnout by pre-vetting a 'Remote Support Stack' of global medical and mental health services before a crisis occurs.
Immediately seek 15-30 minutes of natural morning sunlight upon arrival in a new time zone to provide the most powerful signal for resetting your body's internal clock and combating jet lag.
summary: For global professionals, treating health as a personal preference instead of a core business asset leads to burnout and diminished performance. This guide provides a three-part framework—Systemize, Optimize, and Fortify—to create non-negotiable routines for sleep, nutrition, and cognitive function that work anywhere. By implementing these operational systems, you transform your well-being from a liability into a competitive advantage, ensuring you can operate with sustained energy and clarity on the global stage.
ai_overview_answer: To maintain peak health and performance, global professionals should implement a three-part operational framework: Systemize, Optimize, and Fortify. First, systemize foundational health by creating non-negotiable Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for daily mobility, nutrition, and sleep. This includes establishing a "Sleep Fortress" protocol to control your environment and an "Always-On" nutrition rule of prioritizing protein and fiber to stabilize energy. Next, optimize cognitive output with a specific jet lag mitigation protocol that uses strategic sunlight exposure and meal timing to reset your internal clock. You can further preserve mental energy by ruthlessly eliminating decision fatigue through a work "uniform" and pre-planned meals. Finally, fortify against burnout by building a remote support stack of telehealth services and scheduling a "Deliberate Disconnect" for recovery. Crucially, these health protocols must be proactively blocked in your calendar as non-negotiable appointments to ensure consistency.

itemListElement.name: How to Maintain a Healthy Routine While Traveling

@graph.name: How to Maintain a Healthy Routine While Traveling
@graph.description: Stop guessing. Learn to build a healthy routine while traveling with a system designed for global professionals. Protect your energy, focus, and performance anywhere.

step.name: Systemize Your Foundation: Location-Agnostic Health SOPs

itemListElement.name: The '5-Minute Mobility' SOP
itemListElement.text: The physical toll of long-haul flights and cramped workspaces is real and cumulative. Prolonged sitting stiffens the thoracic spine (your mid-back), which can trigger a cascade of issues in your neck, shoulders, and lower back. This SOP is not a workout; it's a daily system restore designed to counteract that stress. Before you open your laptop, execute a pre-built, equipment-free routine. Simple movements like cat-cow for spinal flexion, book openers for thoracic rotation, and deep squats for hip and ankle mobility can dramatically improve how you feel and function. This is your frontline defense against the physical drag of a demanding travel schedule.

itemListElement.name: The 'Always-On' Nutrition Protocol
itemListElement.text: Maintaining stable energy and cognitive function in a foreign food environment is a significant challenge. This simple, three-rule system removes the guesswork. 1. Protein and Fiber First: At every meal, prioritize protein and fiber. This combination slows the digestion of carbohydrates, preventing the sharp spikes and crashes in blood glucose that kill focus. 2. Set a 'Hydration Trigger': Dehydration, worsened by air travel and caffeine, is a direct threat to mental clarity. Create a simple, unbreakable rule, like drinking a full glass of water before every coffee or meal. 3. Identify Your Universal 'Grocery Staple': Upon arrival in a new city, immediately buy one consistent, healthy food item like Greek yogurt, eggs, or apples. This small action creates an immediate anchor of control and ensures you have a reliable option, reducing decision fatigue from day one.

itemListElement.name: The 'Sleep Fortress' Protocol
itemListElement.text: You cannot control every external noise, but you can control your immediate environment. This non-negotiable checklist turns any room into a sanctuary engineered for deep, restorative sleep. Pack a high-quality sleep mask and earplugs to block disruptive light and sound. Implement a strict 'digital sundown'—no screens for at least one hour before bed—to allow for the natural production of the sleep hormone melatonin. Finally, take command of the thermostat. Most sleep experts agree that the ideal temperature for sleep is between 60-67°F (15.5-19.5°C), as it supports your body's natural nightly drop in core temperature.

itemListElement.name: The 'Calendar Armor' Technique
itemListElement.text: The most common failure point in maintaining health on the road is allowing your schedule to be dictated entirely by external demands. To prevent this, you must treat your well-being as a critical business deliverable. Proactively block 30-60 minutes for these SOPs—mobility, a walk to get sunlight, finding your grocery staple—in your calendar *before* accepting any client meetings. Defend this time with the same commitment you would give a client call. This is a non-negotiable appointment with your 'Chief Performance Officer'—you.

step.name: Optimize for Peak Performance: Your Cognitive Toolkit

itemListElement.name: The 48-Hour Jet Lag Mitigation Protocol
itemListElement.text: When a career-defining meeting is on the line, you need a precise strategy, not generic advice. This protocol begins before you depart. T-24 Hours: Begin shifting your meal times closer to your destination's schedule. Food is a powerful cue for your body's internal clock, or circadian rhythm. During Flight: Hydrate relentlessly with water and avoid alcohol, which disrupts sleep quality. Set your watch to the destination time immediately. Use a sleep mask and earplugs to sleep if it's nighttime there; stay awake if it's daytime. Strategic Caffeine: If traveling east ('losing time'), use caffeine in the morning of your departure. If traveling west ('gaining time'), use it to stay awake closer to your new bedtime. Stop all caffeine at least 8 hours before your destination bedtime. Upon Arrival: Immediately expose yourself to 15-30 minutes of natural morning sunlight without sunglasses. This is the most powerful signal to reset your internal clock. First 24 Hours: Anchor your sleep time. Go to bed at a reasonable hour in the new time zone, even if you're tired earlier. Avoid naps longer than 30 minutes.

itemListElement.name: Managing Decision Fatigue
itemListElement.text: Your brain's capacity for high-quality decisions is a finite resource. Every trivial choice—what to wear, where to find lunch—depletes that reserve, leaving less for the complex problem-solving your clients demand. To preserve this cognitive capital, ruthlessly eliminate non-essential choices. Implement a work 'uniform' of versatile, professional basics. Automate your nutrition by pre-deciding your breakfast and lunch based on your 'grocery staple.' This isn't about restriction; it's about strategically preserving your best mental energy for high-value work.

itemListElement.name: The 'Deep Work' Environmental Setup
itemListElement.text: In unfamiliar environments, you must create your own triggers for focus. This requires a repeatable ritual that signals to your brain that it's time for concentrated effort. The sequence could be: clear your workspace completely, put on noise-canceling headphones, and start a pre-selected productivity playlist. This ritual creates a powerful psychological cue, a conditioned response that helps you bypass procrastination and enter a state of flow, regardless of whether you're in a sterile hotel room or a bustling co-working space.

step.name: Fortify Against Risk: Your Anti-Burnout Protocol

itemListElement.name: Build Your 'Remote Support Stack'
itemListElement.text: Before you face a crisis in an unfamiliar country, have a pre-vetted support system on standby. The goal is to eliminate the scramble and anxiety of finding help under duress. Your stack is a non-negotiable part of your travel pack. A global telehealth service for immediate medical consultations. An international mental health support platform. As psychologist Carolin Müller notes, 'Depression and anxiety don't need a visa.' Acknowledging this reality is the first step in preparing for it. A reliable method for finding accredited, English-speaking doctors in your most frequent destinations.

itemListElement.name: The 'Deliberate Disconnect' Protocol
itemListElement.text: High performers often confuse rest with weakness—a critical error in judgment. To sustain elite output, you must strategically schedule recovery. Block one half-day per week in your calendar that is explicitly 'non-productive.' This time is not for errands or life admin; it is a scheduled investment in your long-term creativity and mental stamina. Use it for activities that are purely for recovery and inspiration—visiting a museum, taking a long walk without a destination, or reading fiction. This protocol refills the cognitive and creative wells that your client work constantly depletes.

itemListElement.name: Architect Your 'Social Scaffolding'
itemListElement.text: The freedom of a global career can quickly curdle into profound professional isolation, a direct threat to your mental health and longevity. You must be ruthlessly intentional about building community. This requires systemizing connection to create a predictable structure for peer feedback and psychological support. Commit to one virtual coffee each week with a trusted peer or mentor. This maintains your connection to your professional network and provides invaluable context. Schedule one in-person meetup each week. Use platforms like Meetup or local co-working space events to connect with peers in your current location. This simple act grounds you in your new environment and combats the transience that fuels loneliness.

mainEntity.name: How do I maintain peak cognitive performance while traveling?

acceptedAnswer.text: Peak cognitive function is the result of a protected system. Your primary strategy is to ruthlessly eliminate decision fatigue and stabilize your physiology. This means leaning on your "Sleep Fortress" Protocol to guarantee restorative rest and your "Always-On" Nutrition Protocol to maintain stable blood sugar. Offload trivial decisions by creating a work "uniform" and pre-deciding meals. Every choice you eliminate frees up mental bandwidth for high-value client work.

mainEntity.name: What is the best routine to beat jet lag for a crucial business meeting?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no magic bullet, only a superior system. For several days pre-flight, gradually shift your bedtime and meal times closer to your destination's schedule. Arrive well-rested, not with a sleep deficit. In-flight, immediately set your watch to the destination time, hydrate relentlessly, and avoid alcohol. On arrival, get outside into natural daylight as soon as possible, especially morning light. This is the most powerful signal to reset your body's clock.

mainEntity.name: How can I build a sustainable health system, not just follow tips?

acceptedAnswer.text: The key is shifting your mindset from reactive "tips" to proactive "Standard Operating Procedures" (SOPs). A system is built once and then executed repeatedly, removing the need for in-the-moment willpower. Start by implementing the non-negotiable protocols in this guide: the "5-Minute Mobility" SOP, the "Always-On" Nutrition Protocol, and the "Sleep Fortress" Protocol. Treat these with the same seriousness as a client deadline. That is the difference between a tip and a system.

mainEntity.name: What are the most effective strategies to prevent burnout as a frequent traveler?

acceptedAnswer.text: Burnout prevention is proactive risk management. The most effective strategies address the core causes: exhaustion, isolation, and a loss of control. Schedule a "Deliberate Disconnect" in your calendar—a non-negotiable block of time for restorative activities. Actively combat professional isolation by scheduling regular virtual calls with your home network and in-person meetups. Finally, have your "Remote Support Stack" pre-vetted and ready before a crisis occurs.

mainEntity.name: How do I create a consistent sleep schedule when constantly changing time zones?

acceptedAnswer.text: Consistency is about ritual, not just timing. While your bedtime will shift, the rituals surrounding it should not. Adhere strictly to your "Sleep Fortress" Protocol every night: eye mask, earplugs, a cool room, and a "digital sundown" with no screens an hour before bed. When you arrive in a new location, immediately adopt the local time. This creates a powerful set of sleep cues that your body learns to respond to, regardless of what the clock says.

mainEntity.name: What are the best low-equipment exercises for a small hotel room?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your goal is consistency, not intensity. The "5-Minute Hotel Room Mobility" SOP is your daily baseline. For a simple and effective workout, focus on compound movements: Bodyweight Squats, Push-Ups (modified on a desk or bed), Planks, and Lunges. Perform 2-3 sets of 10-20 repetitions for each. This circuit can be completed in under 20 minutes and is highly effective for maintaining strength and energy.

mainEntity.name: How do I eat healthy when I only have access to restaurants?

acceptedAnswer.text: This requires a simple, rule-based approach. Follow the "Protein and Fiber First" rule from your nutrition protocol. Build your meal around a lean protein source (grilled fish, chicken, beans) and vegetables. Ask for sauces on the side and request that vegetables be steamed or grilled. Swap starchy sides for an extra serving of vegetables. Having your universal "Grocery Staple" (like nuts or fruit) in your room ensures you always have a healthy option and aren't forced to make poor choices out of hunger.

body.content: Your Health is Your Business: The High-Performance System for Global Professionals

Forget the generic advice for tourists. You're not on vacation; you're an operator. For a Global Professional, a "bad health day" isn't an inconvenience—it's a direct hit to your revenue, reputation, and cognitive output. This guide reframes your well-being from a personal preference into your most critical business asset. We'll provide a 3-part operational framework—**Systemize, Optimize, and Fortify**—to protect your performance, mitigate the catastrophic risk of burnout, and empower you to operate at an elite level, anywhere in the world.
Systemize Your Foundation: Location-Agnostic Health SOPs

High-stakes optimization is only possible on a stable foundation. The first step is to move beyond ad-hoc decisions by establishing personal Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)—repeatable, non-negotiable systems that function whether you're in a hotel in Tokyo or an Airbnb in Lisbon. The goal is to automate the essentials, preserving your cognitive energy for high-value work and creating a baseline of stability in an otherwise unpredictable environment.

items: **The "5-Minute Mobility" SOP:** The physical toll of long-haul flights and cramped workspaces is real and cumulative. Prolonged sitting stiffens the thoracic spine (your mid-back), which can trigger a cascade of issues in your neck, shoulders, and lower back. This SOP is not a workout; it's a daily system restore designed to counteract that stress. Before you open your laptop, execute a pre-built, equipment-free routine. Simple movements like cat-cow for spinal flexion, book openers for thoracic rotation, and deep squats for hip and ankle mobility can dramatically improve how you feel and function. This is your frontline defense against the physical drag of a demanding travel schedule.

**The "Always-On" Nutrition Protocol:** Maintaining stable energy and cognitive function in a foreign food environment is a significant challenge. This simple, three-rule system removes the guesswork.
    1.  **Protein and Fiber First:** At every meal, prioritize protein and fiber. This combination slows the digestion of carbohydrates, preventing the sharp spikes and crashes in blood glucose that kill focus.
    2.  **Set a "Hydration Trigger":** Dehydration, worsened by air travel and caffeine, is a direct threat to mental clarity. Create a simple, unbreakable rule, like drinking a full glass of water before every coffee or meal.
    3.  **Identify Your Universal "Grocery Staple":** Upon arrival in a new city, immediately buy one consistent, healthy food item like Greek yogurt, eggs, or apples. This small action creates an immediate anchor of control and ensures you have a reliable option, reducing decision fatigue from day one.

**The "Sleep Fortress" Protocol:** You cannot control every external noise, but you can control your immediate environment. This non-negotiable checklist turns any room into a sanctuary engineered for deep, restorative sleep. Pack a high-quality sleep mask and earplugs to block disruptive light and sound. Implement a strict "digital sundown"—no screens for at least one hour before bed—to allow for the natural production of the sleep hormone melatonin. Finally, take command of the thermostat. Most sleep experts agree that the ideal temperature for sleep is between 60-67°F (15.5-19.5°C), as it supports your body's natural nightly drop in core temperature.

**The "Calendar Armor" Technique:** The most common failure point in maintaining health on the road is allowing your schedule to be dictated entirely by external demands. To prevent this, you must treat your well-being as a critical business deliverable. Proactively block 30-60 minutes for these SOPs—mobility, a walk to get sunlight, finding your grocery staple—in your calendar *before* accepting any client meetings. Defend this time with the same commitment you would give a client call. This is a non-negotiable appointment with your "Chief Performance Officer"—you.

body.content: Optimize for Peak Performance: Your Cognitive Toolkit

With your foundational systems armored in your calendar, you can shift from defense to offense. This is where we sharpen the spear. Your SOPs protect your baseline capacity, but a cognitive toolkit is what allows you to deliver elite-level work under the intense pressures of tight deadlines and shifting time zones. It’s about having specific protocols to manage the unique stressors of high-stakes travel, ensuring your brain is as ready for the boardroom as your body is for the flight.

items:
**The 48-Hour Jet Lag Mitigation Protocol:** When a career-defining meeting is on the line, you need a precise strategy, not generic advice. This protocol begins before you depart.
    *   **T-24 Hours:** Begin shifting your meal times closer to your destination's schedule. Food is a powerful cue for your body's internal clock, or circadian rhythm.
    *   **During Flight:** Hydrate relentlessly with water and avoid alcohol, which disrupts sleep quality. Set your watch to the destination time immediately. Use a sleep mask and earplugs to sleep if it's nighttime there; stay awake if it's daytime.
    *   **Strategic Caffeine:** If traveling east ("losing time"), use caffeine in the morning of your departure. If traveling west ("gaining time"), use it to stay awake closer to your new bedtime. Stop all caffeine at least 8 hours before your destination bedtime.
    *   **Upon Arrival:** Immediately expose yourself to 15-30 minutes of natural morning sunlight without sunglasses. This is the most powerful signal to reset your internal clock.
    *   **First 24 Hours:** Anchor your sleep time. Go to bed at a reasonable hour in the new time zone, even if you're tired earlier. Avoid naps longer than 30 minutes.

**Managing Decision Fatigue:** Your brain's capacity for high-quality decisions is a finite resource. Every trivial choice—what to wear, where to find lunch—depletes that reserve, leaving less for the complex problem-solving your clients demand. To preserve this cognitive capital, ruthlessly eliminate non-essential choices. Implement a work "uniform" of versatile, professional basics. Automate your nutrition by pre-deciding your breakfast and lunch based on your "grocery staple." This isn't about restriction; it's about strategically preserving your best mental energy for high-value work.

**The "Deep Work" Environmental Setup:** In unfamiliar environments, you must create your own triggers for focus. This requires a repeatable ritual that signals to your brain that it's time for concentrated effort. The sequence could be: clear your workspace completely, put on noise-canceling headphones, and start a pre-selected productivity playlist. This ritual creates a powerful psychological cue, a conditioned response that helps you bypass procrastination and enter a state of flow, regardless of whether you're in a sterile hotel room or a bustling co-working space.

body.content: Fortify Against Risk: Your Anti-Burnout Protocol

Systemizing your foundation and optimizing your output are powerful investments, but they are incomplete without fortifying the enterprise itself: you. Long-term success as a Global Professional is a marathon, and victory is defined by sustainability. Engineering an anti-burnout protocol is about building a resilient operational structure that mitigates the existential business risks of burnout and professional isolation.

items:
**Build Your "Remote Support Stack":** Before you face a crisis in an unfamiliar country, have a pre-vetted support system on standby. The goal is to eliminate the scramble and anxiety of finding help under duress. Your stack is a non-negotiable part of your travel pack.
    *   A global telehealth service for immediate medical consultations.
    *   An international mental health support platform. As psychologist Carolin Müller notes, "Depression and anxiety don't need a visa." Acknowledging this reality is the first step in preparing for it.
    *   A reliable method for finding accredited, English-speaking doctors in your most frequent destinations.

**The "Deliberate Disconnect" Protocol:** High performers often confuse rest with weakness—a critical error in judgment. To sustain elite output, you must strategically schedule recovery. Block one half-day per week in your calendar that is explicitly "non-productive." This time is not for errands or life admin; it is a scheduled investment in your long-term creativity and mental stamina. Use it for activities that are purely for recovery and inspiration—visiting a museum, taking a long walk without a destination, or reading fiction. This protocol refills the cognitive and creative wells that your client work constantly depletes.

**Architect Your "Social Scaffolding":** The freedom of a global career can quickly curdle into profound professional isolation, a direct threat to your mental health and longevity. You must be ruthlessly intentional about building community. This requires systemizing connection to create a predictable structure for peer feedback and psychological support.
    *   **Commit to one virtual coffee each week** with a trusted peer or mentor. This maintains your connection to your professional network and provides invaluable context.
    *   **Schedule one in-person meetup each week.** Use platforms like Meetup or local co-working space events to connect with peers in your current location. This simple act grounds you in your new environment and combats the transience that fuels loneliness.

body.content: Frequently Asked Questions
Building these systems is a significant strategic shift. Here are operational answers to the most common queries from Global Professionals focused on building a resilient, high-performance career.
How do I maintain peak cognitive performance while traveling?

Peak cognitive function is the result of a protected system. Your primary strategy is to ruthlessly eliminate decision fatigue and stabilize your physiology. This means leaning on your "Sleep Fortress" Protocol to guarantee restorative rest and your "Always-On" Nutrition Protocol to maintain stable blood sugar. Offload trivial decisions by creating a work "uniform" and pre-deciding meals. Every choice you eliminate frees up mental bandwidth for high-value client work.
What is the best routine to beat jet lag for a crucial business meeting?

There is no magic bullet, only a superior system. For several days pre-flight, gradually shift your bedtime and meal times closer to your destination's schedule. Arrive well-rested, not with a sleep deficit. In-flight, immediately set your watch to the destination time, hydrate relentlessly, and avoid alcohol. On arrival, get outside into natural daylight as soon as possible, especially morning light. This is the most powerful signal to reset your body's clock.
How can I build a sustainable health *system*, not just follow tips?

The key is shifting your mindset from reactive "tips" to proactive "Standard Operating Procedures" (SOPs). A system is built once and then executed repeatedly, removing the need for in-the-moment willpower. Start by implementing the non-negotiable protocols in this guide: the "5-Minute Mobility" SOP, the "Always-On" Nutrition Protocol, and the "Sleep Fortress" Protocol. Treat these with the same seriousness as a client deadline. That is the difference between a tip and a system.
What are the most effective strategies to prevent burnout as a frequent traveler?

Burnout prevention is proactive risk management. The most effective strategies address the core causes: exhaustion, isolation, and a loss of control. Schedule a "Deliberate Disconnect" in your calendar—a non-negotiable block of time for restorative activities. Actively combat professional isolation by scheduling regular virtual calls with your home network and in-person meetups. Finally, have your "Remote Support Stack" pre-vetted and ready before a crisis occurs.
How do I create a consistent sleep schedule when constantly changing time zones?

Consistency is about ritual, not just timing. While your bedtime will shift, the rituals surrounding it should not. Adhere strictly to your "Sleep Fortress" Protocol every night: eye mask, earplugs, a cool room, and a "digital sundown" with no screens an hour before bed. When you arrive in a new location, immediately adopt the local time. This creates a powerful set of sleep cues that your body learns to respond to, regardless of what the clock says.
What are the best low-equipment exercises for a small hotel room?

Your goal is consistency, not intensity. The "5-Minute Hotel Room Mobility" SOP is your daily baseline. For a simple and effective workout, focus on compound movements: Bodyweight Squats, Push-Ups (modified on a desk or bed), Planks, and Lunges. Perform 2-3 sets of 10-20 repetitions for each. This circuit can be completed in under 20 minutes and is highly effective for maintaining strength and energy.
How do I eat healthy when I only have access to restaurants?

This requires a simple, rule-based approach. Follow the "Protein and Fiber First" rule from your nutrition protocol. Build your meal around a lean protein source (grilled fish, chicken, beans) and vegetables. Ask for sauces on the side and request that vegetables be steamed or grilled. Swap starchy sides for an extra serving of vegetables. Having your universal "Grocery Staple" (like nuts or fruit) in your room ensures you always have a healthy option and aren't forced to make poor choices out of hunger.
Conclusion: You Are the CEO of Your Most Valuable Asset

The ultimate success of your global career hinges on a profound shift in mindset. You are not merely a professional who travels; you are the founder, operator, and chief executive of your own personal enterprise. Your most valuable, non-negotiable asset in this venture is your physical and mental capital. Viewing your well-being through any other lens is a direct threat to your long-term profitability.
Think of your health protocols as distinct corporate divisions:

items: **Your Nutrition Protocol is Supply Chain Management.** It ensures your enterprise has a consistent, high-quality energy source to fuel operations, preventing the costly shutdowns caused by energy crashes and brain fog.

**Your Sleep Protocol is Research & Development.** This is where you synthesize information, restore cognitive function, and generate the creative insights that differentiate you. Defunding this department is a recipe for strategic failure.

**Your Mobility Protocol is Equipment Maintenance.** It ensures your primary physical asset—your body—remains durable, resilient, and capable of withstanding the rigorous demands of your operating environment.

body.content: This is the essence of the **Systemize, Optimize, and Fortify** framework. You **Systemize** to create a baseline of operational stability that functions flawlessly across borders. You **Optimize** to move beyond stability, sharpening your cognitive output to deliver peak performance. Finally, you **Fortify** to manage long-term risk, building the structural supports that defend against burnout and isolation.

By implementing this framework, you transform your well-being from a source of anxiety into a defensible competitive advantage. While others are running on fumes, battling jet lag, and making poor choices out of exhaustion, you will operate with clarity, energy, and control. This is how you ensure your most valuable asset continues to appreciate, no matter where in the world you choose to operate.
